hook: AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to start my morning routine.
caption:
AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to start my morning routine.

I asked for help. I got a Wikipedia article.

Here's the thing nobody talks about: AI doesn't know if you're someone who needs the full system mapped out before you can move — or someone who just needs ONE thing to try tomorrow morning.

Both are valid. They're just completely different brains.

The person who needs the full system feels calm when they see all 47 steps. Structure = safety.

The person who needs one thing? They closed the tab after step 3. Now they feel overwhelmed AND behind.

Same prompt. Same AI. Two completely different experiences of failure.

This is why "just be more specific in your prompts" is bad advice. You shouldn't have to manually explain your entire cognitive style every time you open a chat window.

Your BootFile does that for you. It tells the AI whether you're a Systems Thinker who wants the architecture first — or an Action Taker who needs the smallest possible next step.

The AI doesn't get smarter. It just finally knows who it's talking to.

Which one are you — 47 steps or just tell me what to do first? Drop it below 👇
